hero.body: Strong safety mindset and fast learner. Comfortable working under pressure and following procedures. Looking for an opportunity to learn and grow on the Norwegian shelf — deck, roustabout, rigger or crane-operator positions. Based in Stavern, able to mobilise on short notice.

certs.title: Offshore credentials.
certs.lede: Course certificates and cert cards shown in full below each entry.

also.body1: These are personal tools I built to prepare myself for offshore work: a simulator for overhead-crane lifts, a drilling fundamentals simulator, and a rigging route planner.
also.body2: I enjoy turning new knowledge I pick up from courses into models I can interact with and keep learning from. That habit is how I go from zero to competent fast — and how I make the content stick.

background.title: Transferable experience.
background.divingLine: 1,000+ hours in open-water operations. PADI instructor work across Norway, Philippines, Mexico, Brazil and Honduras — gear checks, briefings and calm decisions under pressure.
background.internationalLine: Lived and worked across five countries. Used to mixed-nationality crews, strict safety procedures, and long working days in remote locations.

GSK.name: GSK — Basic Safety Course Offshore
GSK.description: RSS-20756. Norwegian offshore basic safety — sea survival, fire, first aid and HUET. Valid 03/2030.

G11.name: G11 — Lifting Equipment (Løfteredskap)
G11.description: Rigging and signalling for lifting operations — slings, shackles and lifting gear.

NOG113.name: NOG 113 / NS 9610 — Fall Protection Offshore
NOG113.description: Norwegian Oil & Gas standard for working at height, harness use and rescue.

Rigger.name: Rigger Course (P-2.4 / P-4.4)
Rigger.description: Specialist rigger training covering lift planning, load handling and signalling.

G4.name: G4 — Bridge and Overhead Crane
G4.description: Operator certificate for bridge and overhead (travers) cranes.

G20.name: G20 — Hydraulic Crane
G20.description: Hydraulic crane operator certificate. Course scheduled 15.05.2026.

Truck.name: Truck Operator T1 / T2 / T3 / T4
Truck.description: Certified forklift operator across classes T1 through T4.

additionalTraining.humanFactors: Human Factors in Oil & Gas — Global Network of Companies
additionalTraining.wocrm: IWCF — Well Operations Crew Resource Management

bio1.text: Diving gave me the foundation: {strong} across Norway, the Philippines, Mexico, Brazil and Honduras. Safety and situation awareness became an instinct — gear checks, buddy protocols, decisions that matter. That translates directly to deck work.
bio1.strong: 1,000+ dives as a PADI and CMAS instructor

bio2.text: Three years in software taught me problem-solving under pressure, how to read documentation and learn fast new systems. {strong} as rigs get more instrumented and data-driven.
bio2.strong: Computer literacy is only going to matter more offshore, not less,

bio3.text: Offshore is where I want to be. {strong} Norwegian oil and gas built a lot of what makes this country great, and I want to contribute to it directly.
bio3.strong: Physical work with real stakes. Mental challenges that build strength. A team I depend on and who depends on me.

bio4.text: Certifications in hand: {strong} scheduled for 15 May 2026. Available to mobilise.
bio4.strong: GSK, G11, NOG 113 Fallsikring, Riggerkurs, G4 Traverskran, Truck T1–T2–T3–T4. G20 Hydraulisk Kran

proweb.org: Proweb AS (Webson), Stokke. Full-time. Structured problem-solving, disciplined with procedures and written documentation. Strong computer literacy — adapt quickly to new control systems and digital tools.

dive.org: 1,000+ hours in open-water operations across multiple dive centres. Team leadership under pressure — briefings, risk assessments, pre-operation checks, buddy-check protocols. Emergency first response instructor and oxygen provider.

padi.title: PADI Open Water Scuba Instructor Course and Internship

highSchool.org: Colégio pH, Rio de Janeiro, Brazil

contact.body: Enquiries for offshore deck, rigger and crane-operator positions welcome — rotation, day-rate or longer hitches. I reply within a working day, same day during Norwegian office hours. For time-sensitive mobilisations, phone is faster than email.
contact.emailLede: Role descriptions, rotation details, CV requests.
contact.basedLede: Central European Time. Mobilisation radius: Norwegian shelf and wider North Sea.
contact.availLede: GSK valid to 03/2030, offshore medical valid to 03/2028. Able to mobilise on short notice.
contact.linkedinLede: Work history, endorsements and mutual connections.

tools.lede: Tools I built while preparing for offshore work. Each one turns a course I took into something I can interact with and keep learning from.
tools.backPrefix: The primary offer is offshore work.

cv.fullName: Bruno Bernardo Rossigneux de Oliveira Bueno
cv.profileLead: I have a strong safety mindset and perform well in physically and mentally demanding environments.
cv.profileEmphasis: Dedicated, fast learner, always focused on improving.
cv.profileTail: Comfortable working under pressure and following procedures — looking for an opportunity to learn and grow in an industry that I'm fascinated about.

projects.desktopOnlyNotice: These tools were built with desktop in mind and may not work well on mobile.

overhead-crane-simulator.title: Overhead-crane rig simulator
overhead-crane-simulator.blurb: A simple crane simulator with accurate load physics. Built to keep practicing after the G4 course ended.
overhead-crane-simulator.what: A simple crane simulator with accurate load physics. Built to keep practicing after the G4 course ended — when I no longer have access to a real crane but still want to sharpen the feel. Avoiding swings, stopping swings, anticipation, reading momentum. A way to build muscle memory and experience without consequences.
overhead-crane-simulator.taught: Writing the physics forced me to understand why loads behave the way they do. And once it worked, it became the thing I actually use — a place to keep the feel alive between courses, where mistakes cost nothing.
overview: A browser-based crane simulator I built with accurate load physics. I use it to keep practicing after the G4 course ended — when I no longer had access to a real crane but still wanted to sharpen the feel.

My focus was the behaviour of the load: swing, momentum, anticipation, stopping a swing cleanly. Things that only really click with hours on the controls. A place for me to put those hours in, where mistakes cost nothing.
highlights: Accurate load physics — swing, momentum, inertia
Bridge and trolley controls, rope length, variable load weight
Practice stopping swings, reading momentum, anticipating movement
Runs in the browser, no install

drilling-operations-trainer.title: Drilling fundamentals simulator
drilling-operations-trainer.blurb: An interactive way to keep going deeper into drilling fundamentals — well beyond the content covered at IWCF Level 1.
drilling-operations-trainer.what: An interactive way to keep going deeper into drilling fundamentals — well beyond the content covered at IWCF Level 1. Clear visuals, live instruments, interactive controls. A way to work with the concepts instead of just reading about them. Built to turn dense technical content into something I can see, touch and play with.
drilling-operations-trainer.taught: Reading about pit gain, mud weight and pump pressure is one thing — watching them move, and controlling what moves them, is another. This tool has taught me a lot, from building it to using it. I'm always tweaking it to make it more realistic, and the more I build, the more the subject pulls me in.
overview: An interactive drilling fundamentals simulator I built to go deeper into the subject than the IWCF Level 1 curriculum took me. Live instruments, formation-by-formation geology, a pressure window with safe zones and kick/loss limits, and the controls a driller actually works with — WOB, RPM, pump rate, BOP.

I built it to turn dense technical content into something I could see, touch and play with. I drill through formations, watch pore pressure rise, adjust mud weight to stay in the safe window, handle connections, and see what happens when I don't. Guided tutorials walk through the fundamentals, and I'm always tweaking the sim to go further.
highlights: Live instruments: MD/TVD, MW with ECD, PP with fracture gradient, ROP, SPP, pit volume, background gas, torque
Driller controls: weight on bit, rotary speed, pump rate, pumps on/off, make-connection, shut-in / close BOP
Formation geology with pre-drill prognosis — pore pressure ranges and expected behaviour per formation
Pressure window visualization: pore pressure, mud weight, ECD, fracture gradient, safe window and kick/loss zones
Time controls (½x – 4x) and event log for reviewing decisions
Runs in the browser, no install

rigging-route-planner.title: Rigging route planner
rigging-route-planner.blurb: A planning tool for lifts: hoist positions, angles, load weight distribution across anchor points, and obstacle clearance — over, under, and between.
rigging-route-planner.what: A planning tool for lifts: hoist positions, angles, load weight distribution across anchor points, and obstacle clearance — over, under, and between. Built during Riggerkurs and used throughout the planning phase of the course, and praised by the instructor.
rigging-route-planner.taught: Hours of planning with the tool sharpened my visualization of angles, spatial awareness, and instinct for lift geometry — seeing a setup and knowing immediately whether it works.
overview: An interactive planner I built for rigging lifts. I place the load, set hoist positions, define anchor points, and route the load path around obstacles — over, under, and between. The planner shows hoist angles and how weight distributes across the anchors in real time.
I built it during Riggerkurs to plan the course exercises before executing them. The team ran our plans through it, and the instructor praised the tool.
highlights: Interactive lift setup: load, hoists, anchor points, obstacles
Live hoist angle readouts as the geometry changes
Load weight distribution across anchor points
Obstacle handling — over, under, and between
Runs in the browser, no install

footer.byline: Offshore deck, rigger and crane-operator candidate. Norwegian shelf — ready for rotation.
